title: ATAC-seq from nuclei from frozen tissue

guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"d59rm","text":"The Tn5 enzyme used in this experiment was from a locally produced batch from the  Protein Science Facility at the Karolinska Institutet in Stockholm, which is first discussed in Picelli and others (2014). steps: All steps should be performed on ice or at 4 °C .
Materials for the cutting of tissue on dry ice.
Place 20 mg frozen tissue into a pre-chilled 2 ml Dounce containing 1 ml cold 1x HB and let thaw for 00:05:00 .
Pre-chill all Dounces and pestles to 4 °C in a fridge or on ice

Pre-chill all tubes. For each sample you are processing, you will need: (i) One 2 mL 2 ml LoBind Tubes Eppendorf Catalog #0030108078 per sample (ii) Three 1.5 mL LoBind tubes Eppendorf Catalog #022431021 per sample (iii) one PCR tube per sample (iv) One 50 ml Falcon tube Contributed by users for filtration step per sample

Prepare buffers. i) 6x Homogenization Buffer Stable Solution. ii) 6x Homogenization Buffer Unstable Solution. iii) 1x Homogenization Buffer Unstable Solution. iv) ATAC-RSB v) ATAC-RSB + 10% Tween vi) 2X TD buffer vii) 2X DF buffer

i) 6x Homogenization Buffer stable Solution. %s %s Amount Reagents 2.27084 ml 6x Homogenization Buffer stable 3.78 ml 100mM PMSF 0.28 ul 14.3 M B-mercaptoethanol 1/2 tablet Protease inhibitor (cOmplete Mini)

ii) 6x Homogenization Buffer Unstable Solution. %s %s Amount Reagents 2.27084 ml 6x Homogenization Buffer stable 3.78 ml 100mM PMSF 0.28 ul 14.3 M B-mercaptoethanol 1/2 tablet Protease inhibitor (cOmplete Mini) cOmplete™, Mini, EDTA-free (Protease Inhibitor) Roche Catalog ##11836170001)
ii) 1x Homogenization Buffer Unstable Solution. %s %s Amount Reagents 2.166645 ml 6x Homogenization Buffer Unstable Solution 4.16 ml 1 M sucrose 2.6 ul 500 mM EDTA 130 ul 10.00% NP10 6.540755 ml H2O
Tn5 assembly reaction

A B C 1 Primer Sequence Amount 2 Tn5-A primer TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG 16 ul 3 Tn5-rev primer CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT 16 ul Tube A A B C 1 Primer Sequence Amount 2 Tn5-B primer GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG 16 ul 3 Tn5-rev primer CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT 16 ul Tube B 95 °C to 25 °C cooling -0.1°C/second ~1 hour on PCR machine Olgio Annealing program

TN5 Assembly A B 1 Amount Reagent 2 25 ul Tn5 3 15.5 ul Tube A 4 15.5 ul Tube B 5 33 ul 2X DF buffer Tn5 Assembly Mix Room temperature 01:00:00 Tn5 from the Protein Science Facility at the Karolinska Institutet in Stockholm (Picelli et al. ,2014) or Tn5 transposase with Nextera adapters loaded Contributed by users Catalog #UC-Macro-Tn5-Nextera adapter

Add the calculated amount of cells into a 2 mL lobind eppendorf tube with 1 ml ATAC-RSB + 10% Tween. So taking the example above one would take 1.10 ul of nuclei in the 200 µL ATAC-RSB + 10% Tween to the new tube.
Centrifuge nuclei for 00:10:00 900 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Discard most of the supernatant. Leave a bit in the bottom to ensure that there is enough for the reaction and that the pellet stays intact

Add the reaction mix to the pellet and remaining water and resuspend the pellet in the mix A B 1 Amount Reagents 2 25 ul 2X TD buffer 3 16.5 ul 1X PBS (cold) 4 0.5 ul 1% digitonin 5 0.5 ul 10% Tween -20 6 2.5 ul TN5 assembly Reaction Mix
Put the tube into the thermomixer at 1000 rpm, 37 °C, 30 minutes
Take the tubes out and immediately proceed to the column clean up
Use the Zymo DNA clean & concentrator to clean the nuclei Zymo DNA Clean & Concentrator - 5 Zymo Research Catalog #D4014
Can stop here and start the next day if necessary. Leave samples in 4°
Dounce with “A” loose pestle until resistance goes away (~10 strokes). Put the A pestle into the beaker of water
Dounce with “B” tight pestle for 20 strokes. Put the B pestle into the beaker of water
Pour everything from the dounce into a 30 um MACS smartstrainer which is sitting on top of a labelled 50 ml falcon tube sitting in ice. MACS SmartStrainers 30um Miltenyi Biotec Catalog #130-098-458
Let it drip through for 10-15 minutes.

In the most sterile way possible, cut a small piece of tissue, half a pea size or so, and leave it in the petri dish with a marking on the lid, in the dry ice. Weigh it and cut again if needed. Make sure to use the ethanol to clean everything and be careful not to cut yourself.
- Gloves - White warm gloves - Dry ice - Blades and handle - Forceps - Ethanol spray - Cell culture dish
Add 2 mL 1X HB buffer into the dounce, which is sitting in the ice. Add 0.2 µL 1 M DTT and and 1 µL 100X Halt protease inhibitor
Transfer to a labelled 2 mL Lobind Eppendorf tube, already cold from sitting in ice.
To pellet the nuclei, centrifuge 900 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Discard supernatant
Resuspend the nuclei in 200 µL of ATAC-RSB + 10% Tween
Count the amount of cells using the cell counter in the cell lab.
Calculate the amount of nuclei to use for the next step; (500000 nuclei)
Turn on the thermomixer to 37 °C
Put 10 µL Trypton blue onto a piece of parafilm and mix with 10 µL of the sample
Take 10 µL of the mix and pipette into a cell counting cell
Measure on the cell counting machine, making sure to adjust for the smaller size of the nuclei. The machine will think they are dead cells.
ATAC-seq requires 50,000 cells for the experiment. So for example if the number of nuclei is; 4.76 x 10 7 then the calculation will look like this; 50000 / 47600 = 1.10

Here one can also save the nuclei for later. Simply spin the tube down with the new tube of diluted nuclei, discard the supernatant, add 300 µL of the and put in the -20° freezer. 00:10:00 900 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Add 2-7 volumes of the DNA binding buffer to each volume of DNA sample So in this case the volume is approximately 50 µL , so add 300 µL of DNA binding buffer
Transfer mixture to a provided Zymo-spin column in a collection tube
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds Discard supernatant
Add 200 µL DNA wash buffer to the column Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds
repeat the wash step
Trasnfer the column to a 1.5 low-bind eppendorf tube. Add 21 µL 2 DNA Elution buffer to the column. Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds

Set up PCR; A B 1 Amount Reagent 2 2.5 ul Primer AD1 3 2.5 ul Primer AD2.# 4 25 ul NEBNext Master Mix 5 20 ul sample PCR NEBNext High-Fidelity 2X PCR Master Mix - 250 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #M0541L
Proceed immediately to the qPCR amplification to determine additional cycles step
Remove PCR tubes from the machine and put immediately onto ice.

PCR program - ATAC-seq pre-amplification A B C 1 Tempurature Time Cycle 2 72° 5 minutes 1 3 98° 30 seconds - 4 98° 10 seconds 5 5 63° 30 seconds - 6 72° 1 minute - 7 72 5 minutes 1 8 4 infinate - ATAC-seq pre-amplification

qPCR amplification to determine additional cycles prepare a mix or master mix A B C 1 Reagent 1X 6.5X 2 Molecular grade water 3.76 ul 24.5 ul 3 Primer AD1 0.5 ul 6.5 ul 4 Primer AD2.# 0.5 ul 0.5 ul * 5 25x SYBR green (in DMSO) 0.24 ul 1.56 ul 6 2x NEBNext Master Mix 5 ul 32.5 ul qPCR mix SYBR™ Green I Nucleic Acid Gel Stain - 10,000X concentrate in DMSO Thermo Fisher Catalog #S7563 NEBNext High-Fidelity 2X PCR Master Mix - 250 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #M0541L
Determine the required amount of cycles that each sample needs in addition
qPCR program A B C 1 Tempurature Time Cycle 2 98° 30 seconds 1 3 98° 10 seconds 20 4 63° 30 seconds - 5 72° 1 minute - qPCR program
qPCR extra setup options - 6 machine not 6 - standard - SYBR green - no melt curve - turn off ROX - Fill in sample list
Continue the PCR with the additional amount of cycles skipping the beginning parts of the program
Take the tubes out and immediately proceed to the column clean up
Looking at the final amplification curve, determine the max fluorescence where the graph plateaus
Determine 1/3 of that number. For example if the max is 1400000 then 1/3 would be 433333
Check the graph for how many cycles line up with this number from the curve. In the case above it would be 6 because of where the line was
# of addtional cycles A B C 1 Tempurature Time Cycle 2 98° 10 seconds Based on calculation 3 63° 30 seconds - 4 72° 1 minute - 5 4° Infinate 1 Additional cycles
Use the Zymo DNA Clean & Concentrator to clean the nuclei Zymo DNA Clean & Concentrator - 5 Zymo Research Catalog #D4014
Quality control TapeStation (DS1000) and Qubit quantification
Add 2-7 volumes of the DNA binding buffer to each volume of DNA sample So in this case the volume is approximately 50 ul, so add 300 ul of DNA binding buffer
Transfer mixture to a provided Zymo-spin column in a collection tube
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds Discard supernatant
Add 200 µL DNA wash buffer to the column Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds
Repeat the wash step
Transfer the column to a 1.5 low-bind Eppendorf tube. Add 21 ul Sterile water buffer to the column. Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds
iii) ATAC-RSB: %s %s Amount Reagents 500ul 1M Tris-Hcl ph 7.5 100ul 5M NaCl 150ul 1M MgCl2 49.25ml H2O
iv) ATAC-RSB + 10% Tween %s %s Amount Reagents 8 ml ATAC-RSB 8 ul 10% Tween
v) 2X TD buffer %s %s Amount Reagents 2 ml 1 M Tris-Hcl pH 7.5 1 ml 1 M MgCl2 20 ml 100% Dimethyl Formamide (before the addition of dimethyl formamide, adjust the pH to 7.6 with 100% acetic acid)
vi) 2X DF buffer %s %s Amount Reagents 100 mM HEPES (pH 7.2) 200 mM NaCl 0.2 mM EDTA 2 mM DTT 2.00% Triton X-100 20.00% Glycerol
